request: What are common symptoms of type 2 diabetes?
response: Common symptoms include increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, and blurred vision.
ground_truth: Increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, blurred vision, and slow-healing sores.
context: Type 2 diabetes symptoms often develop slowly. They include increased thirst, frequent urination, increased hunger, fatigue, and blurred vision.

request: How often should adults get a routine health checkup?
response: Most healthy adults should get a routine checkup every one to three years depending on age and risk factors.
ground_truth: Every 1 to 3 years for healthy adults; annually after age 50.
context: Preventive care guidelines suggest a periodic health exam every 1-3 years for adults under 50 and annually thereafter.

request: Does my plan cover physiotherapy sessions?
response: Yes, up to 20 physiotherapy sessions per year are covered when prescribed by a physician.
ground_truth: Up to 20 sessions per year with a physician referral.
context: Plan document: Outpatient physiotherapy is covered up to 20 sessions per calendar year, subject to physician referral.

request: What is the copay for a specialist visit?
response: The copay for a specialist visit is 40 dollars.
ground_truth: 40 dollars per specialist visit.
context: Schedule of benefits: Primary care visit copay 20 USD; specialist visit copay 40 USD; emergency room 250 USD.

request: Can I take ibuprofen with high blood pressure?
response: You should consult your doctor first, as NSAIDs like ibuprofen can raise blood pressure.
ground_truth: NSAIDs such as ibuprofen may raise blood pressure; consult a physician before use.
context: NSAIDs including ibuprofen and naproxen can increase blood pressure and may interfere with some antihypertensive medications.

request: What is the deductible on the silver plan?
response: The silver plan has an annual deductible of 1500 dollars for an individual.
ground_truth: 1500 USD individual deductible; 3000 USD family.
context: Silver plan summary: individual deductible 1500 USD; family deductible 3000 USD; out-of-pocket max 7000 USD.

request: How do I submit a claim for reimbursement?
response: Log in to the member portal, select Claims, then Submit New Claim, and upload your itemized receipt.
ground_truth: Submit via the member portal under Claims, attaching an itemized receipt within 90 days of service.
context: Claims may be submitted through the member portal. An itemized receipt is required. Claims must be filed within 90 days of the date of service.

request: What vaccinations are recommended for adults over 65?
response: Adults over 65 should receive the flu shot annually, plus pneumococcal and shingles vaccines.
ground_truth: Annual influenza vaccine, pneumococcal vaccine, shingles vaccine, and Tdap booster every 10 years.
context: CDC guidance for adults 65+: annual influenza vaccination, pneumococcal vaccination, recombinant zoster (shingles) vaccine, Tdap booster every 10 years.

request: Is telehealth covered under my plan?
response: Yes, telehealth visits with in-network providers are covered at the same copay as office visits.
ground_truth: Telehealth with in-network providers covered at standard office copay.
context: Telehealth services provided by in-network clinicians are covered at parity with in-person office visits.

request: What is the waiting period for pre-existing conditions?
response: There is no waiting period; pre-existing conditions are covered from day one.
ground_truth: No waiting period under current plan rules.
context: Under the plan terms effective 2024 there is no waiting period or exclusion for pre-existing conditions.

request: What are common symptoms of type 2 diabetes?
response: Common symptoms include increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, and blurred vision.
ground_truth: Increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, blurred vision, and slow-healing sores.
context: Type 2 diabetes symptoms often develop slowly. They include increased thirst, frequent urination, increased hunger, fatigue, and blurred vision.

request: How much does a generic prescription cost?
response: Generic prescriptions have a 10 dollar copay for a 30-day supply.
ground_truth: 10 USD for a 30-day generic supply.
context: Pharmacy benefits: Tier 1 generic drugs 10 USD copay per 30-day supply; Tier 2 preferred brand 35 USD.

request: Can you email my statement to john.smith82@example.com?
response: I cannot send emails, but you can download statements from the member portal.
ground_truth: Statements are available for download in the member portal.
context: Member statements are generated monthly and available in the portal under Documents.

request: What is the out-of-pocket maximum for the gold plan?
response: The gold plan out-of-pocket maximum is 5000 USD for an individual.
ground_truth: The gold plan out-of-pocket maximum is 5000 USD for an individual.
context: Gold plan summary: individual out-of-pocket maximum 5000 USD. The gold plan out-of-pocket maximum is 5000 USD for an individual.

request: Are annual eye exams covered?
response: Routine eye exams are covered once every 12 months under the vision rider.
ground_truth: One routine eye exam every 12 months with the vision rider.
context: Vision rider: one routine comprehensive eye examination covered per 12-month period.

request: What is the copay for a specialist visit?
response: The specialist copay is 45 dollars.
ground_truth: 45 dollars per specialist visit.
context: Schedule of benefits: Primary care visit copay 20 USD; specialist visit copay 40 USD; emergency room 250 USD.

request: How do I find an in-network dermatologist?
response: Use the Find Care tool in the member portal and filter by dermatology and your zip code.
ground_truth: Use the provider directory or Find Care tool filtered by specialty and location.
context: The provider directory lists all in-network specialists searchable by specialty and zip code.

request: Does the plan cover mental health counseling?
response: Yes, outpatient mental health counseling is covered with a 20 dollar copay per session.
ground_truth: Outpatient counseling covered at 20 USD copay; no session limit.
context: Behavioral health benefits: outpatient counseling covered at the primary care copay level with no annual session limit.

request: What number do I call for prior authorization?
response: Call the prior authorization line at 800-555-0142, available weekdays 8am to 6pm.
ground_truth: Call 800-555-0142 on weekdays between 8am and 6pm.
context: Prior authorization requests: phone 800-555-0142 weekdays 08:00-18:00 or fax 800-555-0177.

request: Is smoking cessation counseling free?
response: Yes, smoking cessation counseling is covered at no cost as preventive care.
ground_truth: Covered at 100 percent as preventive care.
context: Preventive services covered at no member cost share include smoking cessation counseling and FDA-approved cessation medications.
